title: What time is it?
pedagogy: Vocabulary: กี่ (mid ก + mai ek → low), โมง (low ม + live ง → mid). Pattern: กี่ + classifier asks 'how many' — กี่โมง what time, กี่บาท how many baht. Concept: the Thai six-hour clock (โมง day / ทุ่ม night), presented receptively.

exercises.title: The clock resets four times a day
body: Officially Thailand uses the 24-hour clock. Out loud, it runs in six-hour blocks: โมง carries the daytime hours, ทุ่ม takes the night shift.
You don't need to master the blocks yet. กี่โมง — “how many hours?” — asks the time in every one of them.
กี่ is the counting question word: กี่โมง what time, กี่บาท how many baht, กี่คน how many people. Always with a unit after it.

exercises.explanation: กี่โมงคะ — how-many-hours? A question, so the high-tone คะ closes it.

exercises.context: You're a man asking the hostel desk what time checkout is.
exercises.question: How do you ask “what time?”

choices.thai: กี่โมงครับ
กี่วันครับ

exercises.explanation: กี่โมงครับ — counted hours plus the particle. Bare กี่โมง is friends-only, and กี่วันครับ asks “how many days?” — a different vacation problem.
